references.text: Coritico, FP, PJF Orias, MS Acola, AJD Lagumbay, PW Fritsch & VB Amoroso. 2026. Plagiogyria longipinnata (Plagiogyriaceae), a new fern species from Mount Redondo, Dinagat Island, Philippines. Phytotaxa 737: 191-198.
Zhang XC & HP Nooteboom. 1997. Some new combinations in Plagiogyria (Plagiogyriaceae, Pteridophyta). Blumea 42: 483-484
Zhang XC & HP Nooteboom. 1998. A taxonomic revision of Plagiogyriaceae (Pteridophyta). Blumea 43: 401-469
Zhang XC & HP Nooteboom. 1998. Plagiogyriaceae. Flora Malesiana series 2, 3: 295-316

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Julie F. Barcelona & Pieter B. Pelser, 10 June 2026

species.citation: (Blume) Bedd. , Ferns Brit. India (1865) t.51 ; Zhang & Nooteboom, Fl. Males. ser. 2, 3 (1998) 300; ≡ Lomaria adnata Blume , Enum. Pl. Javae (1828) 205.
species.distribution: Bhutan, Borneo, China, India, Japan, Java, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Abra, Bataan, Ilocos Norte, Kalinga, Mountain Province, NEGROS.

species.citation: (Baker) Copel. , J. Straits Branch Roy. Asiat. Soc. 63 (1912) 72 ; Zhang & Nooteboom, Fl. Males. ser. 2, 3 (1998) 302; ≡ Lomaria egenolfioides Baker , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1894 (1894) 1. var. latipinna (Copel.) X.C.Zhang & Noot., Blumea 42 (1997) 484; ≡ Plagiogyria tuberculata var. latipinna Copel., PJS 2c (1907) 133. = Plagiogyria tuberculata Copel., PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 153. Distribution: Borneo, Moluccas, Philippines, Sulawesi. LUZON: Apayao (photos), Kalinga, Mountain Province, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao, Davao del Sur, Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS. Notes: 1200-4000m. Native.
species.distribution: LUZON: Apayao (photos), Kalinga, Mountain Province, Quezon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao, Davao del Sur, Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS.

species.citation: (Kunze) Mett. , Abh. Senckenberg. Naturf. Ges. 2 (1858) 274 ; Zhang & Nooteboom, Fl. Males. ser. 2, 3 (1998) 308; ≡ Lomaria euphlebia Kunze , Bot. Zeitung (Berlin) 6 (1848) 521. = Plagiogyria christii Copel. , PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 153 ; Pescuela et al., PJS 153 (2024) 665.
species.distribution: China, India, Japan, Korea, Myanmar, Nepal, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Taiwan, Vietnam. LUZON: Abra, Benguet, Ifugao, Laguna, Mountain Province, Quezon, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao, Davao del Sur, Misamis Oriental, South Cotabato, MINDORO.

species.citation: Copel. , PJS 2c (1907) 133 ; Zhang & Nooteboom, Fl. Males. ser. 2, 3 (1998) 311.
species.distribution: China, Philippines, Taiwan. LUZON: Ilocos Norte, MINDORO.

species.citation: (Blume) Mett. , Abh. Senckenberg. Naturf. Ges. 2 (1858) 273 ; Zhang & Nooteboom, Fl. Males. ser. 2, 3 (1998) 311; Galope-Obemio et al., J. Threatened Taxa 14 (2022) 22047; ≡ Lomaria glauca Blume , Enum. Pl. Javae (1828) 204. = Plagiogyria nana Copel. , PJS 4c (1909) 114; ≡ Plagiogyria glauca var. nana (Copel.) C.Chr. , Brittonia 2 (1937) 284.
species.distribution: Bismarck Arch, Borneo, China, India, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, Myanmar, New Guinea, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Tibet. LUZON: Apayao (photos), Benguet, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Kalinga, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao, Davao del Sur, South Cotabato.

species.citation: (Kunze) Mett. , Abh. Senckenberg. Naturf. Ges. 2 (1858) 272 ; Zhang & Nooteboom, Fl. Males. ser. 2, 3 (1998) 314; Pescuela et al., PJS 153 (2024) 665; ≡ Lomaria pycnophylla Kunze , Bot. Zeitung (Berlin) 6 (1848) 143. = Plagiogyria pycnophylla var. mixta Copel. , PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 154; Type: Copeland 1854, Luzon: Lepanto, monte Data.
species.distribution: Bhutan, Borneo, China, India, Java, Myanmar, Nepal, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Tibet, Vietnam. LUZON: Abra, Benguet, Ifugao, Kalinga, Kalinga-Apayao, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Misamis Oriental.

species.citation: (Hance) Diels , Nat. Pflanzenfam. (Engler & Prantl) 1(4) (1899) 282 ; Zhang & Nooteboom, Fl. Males. ser. 2, 3 (1998) 315; ≡ Blechnum stenopterum Hance , J. Bot. 21 (1883) 268.
species.distribution: China, Japan, Philippines, Taiwan, Vietnam. LUZON: Kalinga, Mountain Province.
